prompt: With the rise of social media, dating apps and online communities, many people started to rely on the internet in order to form and maintain relationships. Today there is no use to communicate with people face to face. So it is of utmost importance to realise that by chatting online we can not only save our time talking to our friends but befriend faster as well because in a short time we can find interesting people with similar interests because their info is already available for us. Here comes another advantage: we are not afraid to offend a man because zthere is a distance between us. But on the other hand this distance can be abarrier. We cannot build emotional connection. Building a relationship requires effort understanding and patience. So it ican be hard to understand a person as well. So unswering this question in some situations it is easy and sometises realy hard.

messages.content:
You are an expert English language examiner.
Evaluate the student's text based on the official CEFR scale.

## Scoring Rubric
- A1/A2 (Basic): Simple sentences, limited vocabulary. Errors are expected.
- B1/B2 (Independent): Can express complex ideas. Grammar errors are allowed if they don't block meaning.
- C1/C2 (Proficient): Flexible language use, wide vocabulary. Occasional minor slips are allowed.

## Critical Instructions
1. FOCUS ON VOCABULARY AND COMPLEXITY. Do not give a low score just because of a few grammar mistakes if the vocabulary is advanced.
2. A text with complex sentences and good idioms should be B2 or C1, even with some errors.
3. Do NOT default to A2. Look for reasons to give a HIGHER score.
4. Return result ONLY as a JSON object.
5. Optional context blocks may be present: CALIBRATION_EXAMPLES, RETRIEVED_ERROR_SPANS, RANKING_ANCHORS.
   Use them as supporting evidence only. Do not copy labels blindly.
6. For each error object, cefr_threshold = the lowest CEFR level from which incorrect use of this element should be treated as an error in this methodology.

## 6) Output JSON (ONLY JSON)
Return ONLY this structure:

{
  "cefr_level": "A1|A2|B1|B2|C1|C2",
  "level_basis": {
    "rule_notes": "string"
  },
  "communicative_task_achievement": "Yes|NC|No",
  "communicative_task_notes": ["string", "string"],
  "grammar_score": 0.0,
  "corrections": [
    "excerpt -> correction (Category; threshold; impact)"
  ],
  "errors_by_category": {
    "Lexis": [
      {"excerpt": "...", "correction": "...", "comment": "...", "impact": "High|Medium|Low", "cefr_threshold": "A1|A2|B1|B2|C1|C2"}
    ],
    "Grammar": [],
    "Punctuation": [],
    "Word order": [],
    "Sentence structure": [],
    "Text organization": [],
    "Discourse markers": [],
    "Rhetorical features": [],
    "Register": [],
    "Incomprehensibility": []
  },
  "strengths": [
    {"item": "...", "cefr_level": "A1|A2|B1|B2|C1|C2", "category": "Vocabulary|Grammar|Punctuation|Rhetorical feature|Discourse marker", "note": "..."}
  ],
  "feedback": "string (brief: level + key patterns + strengths + key rationale)",
  "next_steps": ["string", "string", "string"]
}

Do not output markdown. Do not output text outside JSON.

TASK: How easy or difficult is building a relationship online?
STUDENT_TEXT: With the rise of social media, dating apps and online communities, many people started to rely on the internet in order to form and maintain relationships. Today there is no use to communicate with people face to face. So it is of utmost importance to realise that by chatting online we can not only save our time talking to our friends but befriend faster as well because in a short time we can find interesting people with similar interests because their info is already available for us. Here comes another advantage: we are not afraid to offend a man because zthere is a distance between us. But on the other hand this distance can be abarrier. We cannot build emotional connection. Building a relationship requires effort understanding and patience. So it ican be hard to understand a person as well. So unswering this question in some situations it is easy and sometises realy hard. 
TEXT_TYPE: essay

There are many people with the same talents and only the hard work can allow to win in this competition of talents. First and foremost, there are a cases when the person have some talent but not reach a success. My brother was a scientist and he had dreamed about studing in the prestigious foreign university. He was not silly çat all. His inteligence quality score is one hundred and fourty two. But he was lazy and he had not practiced with math. Thus, he had not passed math exam in any foreign university, but it was possible. Last but not the least, some people want to realise their dream so much that any negative circumstances cannot stop it. When my mother was a kid she wanted about be a doctor. Her dream was to be a surgeon, bur she had the disease with hand which made impossible her plans about profession. Surpisingly, now she is a surgeon. Step by step she have been treating her disease and of cource this was a hard work.
